_notes.purpose: Questions for the First Mapping Assessment. Written in warm, first-person experiential language. No IFS jargon shown to users. Phase 1 responses drive backend inference for cluster weighting — never shown as classifications. Cluster questions surface the pattern; the Naming Moment names the part.
_notes.response_types: phase1_q1: free_text | phase1_q2: free_text | phase1_q3: single_choice | phase1_q4: conditional_yes_no + free_text | phase1_q5: single_choice | clusters: slider_1_to_5 with optional free_text at end of each cluster | phase3: dynamic_generated

phase1.label: The Moment
phase1.intro: Before we look at patterns, let's start with something specific. Think of a recent moment — in the last week or two — when you had a strong reaction to something. It doesn't have to be dramatic. Just a moment where something in you shifted.

questions.text: Briefly describe what happened — just a sentence or two is enough.
questions.placeholder: Something came up at work, a conversation with someone, a feeling out of nowhere...

questions.text: Where did you feel it in your body first?
questions.placeholder: Chest, stomach, shoulders, throat — anywhere it landed...

questions.text: When it hit, what did you do — or want to do?

options.label: I pulled back, got quiet, or wanted to disappear
I said something, pushed back, or got reactive
I got busy, distracted myself, or pushed through
I went a bit flat or disconnected from it
I tried to solve it, manage it, or control the situation
Something else

questions.text: Afterward, was there a second voice — something that commented on how you handled it?

options.label: Yes
No
Not sure

questions.conditional_question: What did it say — even roughly?
questions.conditional_placeholder: Something like 'you overreacted' or 'you should have said more'...

questions.text: How often does something like this happen for you?

options.label: Rarely — this was unusual
Sometimes — a few times a month
Often — a few times a week
Very often — it's basically the background noise of my life

phase1.transition: Thank you for sharing that. Now let's look at some broader patterns — the ones that show up not just in one moment, but across your life.

clusterA.label: Standards & Effort
clusterA.theme: The part that holds the bar — and keeps raising it.
clusterA.intro: These questions are about the internal experience of effort, standards, and what happens when you fall short.

questions.text: When you're working on something that matters to you, there's often a part watching — evaluating how well you're doing. How present is that for you?
When you finish something — a project, a conversation, a task — how quickly can you let yourself feel like it was good enough?
questions.max_label: Rarely — there's always something I should have done better

questions.text: If you make a mistake — especially one others might see — what happens inside?
questions.min_label: I notice it and move on
questions.max_label: It stays with me — I replay it, pick it apart

questions.text: Is there a sense that if you relaxed your standards — even a little — something would go wrong?
In your own words — what does the part of you that monitors your performance feel like it's trying to protect you from?
questions.placeholder: Failure, humiliation, being seen as less than, letting people down...

clusterB.label: Relief & Escape
clusterB.theme: The part that finds a way out when things get to be too much.
clusterB.intro: These questions are about what happens when pressure builds — and how you find relief.

questions.text: When stress or emotional pressure reaches a certain point, there's usually something you reach for — a behavior, a habit, a way of getting space. How familiar is that pattern for you?
questions.min_label: Not very — I generally sit with it
questions.max_label: Very familiar — there's a reliable go-to

questions.text: When you use that relief strategy — whatever it is — how much of it feels like a choice versus something that just happens?
questions.max_label: It happens before I've decided to do it

questions.text: Afterward — after you've gotten that relief — is there usually a part that has an opinion about it?
questions.max_label: Yes — there's criticism, regret, or a cycle that starts up

questions.text: How long has this pattern been with you?

options.label: It developed in the last few years
It's been around for quite a while
For as long as I can remember
I'm not sure

questions.text: What do you think this part of you is actually trying to do — when it reaches for relief?
questions.placeholder: Get a break, feel something different, not feel anything, survive...

clusterC.label: Connection & Relationships
clusterC.theme: The part that manages how safe it is to be close.
clusterC.intro: These questions are about what happens in relationships — especially when there's tension, need, or the possibility of disappointment.

questions.text: When someone you care about is upset — with you, with something, with anyone — what happens inside you?
questions.max_label: Something in me mobilizes — to fix it, smooth it, or move away

questions.text: Is there a part of you that keeps track of how others seem to be feeling toward you — picking up signals, monitoring the temperature?
questions.min_label: Not really — I don't track it closely
questions.max_label: Yes — it's like a background sensor that's always running

questions.text: When you need something from someone — support, space, help — how easy is it to ask directly?
questions.min_label: Pretty easy — I ask when I need to
questions.max_label: Hard — I often manage without asking, or hint rather than ask

questions.text: In conflict or tension with someone close, what does the part of you that wants to preserve the connection tend to do?

options.label: It accommodates — gives way to keep the peace
It turns toward the other person — focuses on their feelings
It pulls back — distances until things cool down
It minimizes — acts like it's not a big deal
It manages the situation — tries to steer the outcome
A mix of several of these

questions.text: What do you think this part is most afraid would happen if it stopped doing its job in relationships?
questions.placeholder: The relationship would fall apart, people would leave, conflict would spiral...

clusterD.label: The Voice Inside
clusterD.theme: The part that comments on you — and what it might be covering.
clusterD.intro: These questions are about the internal commentary — the voice that evaluates, judges, or sometimes goes very quiet.

questions.text: Is there a voice inside that comments on you — on how you're doing, what you should have said, whether you're getting it right?
questions.min_label: Barely — I don't hear it much

questions.text: When that voice speaks, what's its general tone?

options.label: Harsh — critical, contemptuous, disappointed
Worried — anxious, vigilant, scanning for problems
Comparative — measuring me against others or a standard
Coaching — pushing me to do better, work harder
It's more of an absence — things go flat or numb
A mix

questions.text: When that voice is active, what do you tend to do — on the inside?
questions.min_label: I engage with it — argue back, comply, try to satisfy it
questions.max_label: I shut down — go quiet, go flat, try to disappear from myself

questions.text: If you turn toward the harshest thing this voice says — not to agree with it, just to notice it — what does it say about you?
questions.placeholder: That I'm not enough, that I'm a disappointment, that something is wrong with me...

questions.text: There's often something underneath that voice — something it's been trying to protect you from feeling. How much do you sense something there?
questions.min_label: Not really — it's just a voice

clusterD._note_on_exile: If cD_q3 or cD_q5 score above exile_node_placement_threshold, place a shadowed Exile node on the map with NO naming prompt. Label: 'Unknown — waiting to be known'. This is the first planted cliffhanger.

phase3.label: The Connections
phase3.intro: You've met a few parts now. Before we show you what this looks like, there are a couple of questions about how they relate to each other.
phase3._note: Questions in Phase 3 are dynamically generated using the names the user chose in their naming moments. Replace [partA_name], [partB_name], [partC_name] with actual user-chosen names. Select the most clinically relevant pairing based on cluster types revealed.

dynamic_questions.template: [partA_name] and [partB_name] — do they ever seem to be responding to the same thing? Like [partA_name] tries to hold something together, and when that doesn't work, [partB_name] shows up?
dynamic_questions.max_label: Yes — they seem to be in the same system

dynamic_questions.template: Does [partA_name] ever seem to react to [partB_name]? Like one of them triggers or activates the other?
When you think about [partA_name], [partB_name], and [partC_name] all together — how do you feel toward them as a group?
dynamic_questions.placeholder: Tired, grateful, frustrated, curious, something else...
dynamic_questions._note: This is the Self-access baseline. Stored in self_energy_checkins. Never scored or labeled. Used as opening orientation data only.
